0.q: TPS activity in the presence of Mg2+ with glucose-6-phosphate (G6P) as the glycosyl acceptor and UDPG or ADPG as the glycosyl donor forming trehalose 6-phosphate was confirmed.

1.q: TPP activity, i.e., trehalose formation from trehalose-6-phosphate (T6P), for the purified monomeric Saci_0016 (27 kDa) via thin-layer chromatography (TLC) was confirmed.

2.q: The trehalose content compared to that of the parental strain MW001 was decreased in the ΔtreY single mutant, and no trehalose was detected in the triple mutant. Hence, all three pathways contribute to the overall trehalose content.

3.q: Results showed the significance of both pathways for trehalose synthesis and confirmed that the TreT pathway, which had only been predicted so far, is operative in vivo.

4.q: Deletion of saUspA led to premature production of the archaellin FlaB in S. acidocaldarius although motility was not affected.

5.q: SaUspA did not hydrolyze ATP, but stimulated the phosphatase activity of PP2A and might in this manner affect many other processes.
